title: THE REDUCTION OF AMMONIUM MOLYBDATE BY LSODIUM  HYDROGEN ASCORBATE IN THE DARK.
author: S.K. BHATTACHARYYA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The reduction of ammonium molybdate by I-sodium hydrogen ascorbate has been studied in the dark. It has been found that when the concentration of sodium hydrogen ascorbate and ammonium molybdate are equivalent, the velocity of readies is a maximum. For excess concentrations of either reagent, the velocity is less. This indicates that the velocity of reaction is proportional to the concentration of the complex formed by the association of an equivalent of ascorbate with one equivalent of molybdate.
steps_raw: The reduction of ammonium molybdate by I-sodium hydrogen ascorbate has been studied in the dark. It has been found that when the concentration of sodium hydrogen ascorbate and ammonium molybdate are equivalent, the velocity of readies is a maximum. For excess concentrations of either reagent, the velocity is less. This indicates that the velocity of reaction is proportional to the concentration of the complex formed by the association of an equivalent of ascorbate with one equivalent of molybdate.
